content: Existing Self-Refine frameworks for LLMs, which rely on single-perspective iterative self-feedback to refine outputs without extra training, can be enhanced by integrating multiple inconsistent self-reflection perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanism research to eliminate blind spots in single-perspective self-criticism. This creates a completely training-free multi-perspective self-refinement framework that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of no additional data or training while improving output quality over both single-perspective Self-Refine and existing multi-perspective reflection methods that require fine-tuning.
